reasoning: A 5% daily drop in the S&P 500 is a rare event, mostly occurring during extreme market distress (e.g., 2008, 2020). During normal years, the probability of a 5% drop is very low. Even if a 5% drop already occurred in early 2025, the base rate of multiple extreme crashes in a single year outside of acute crises like a pandemic or a massive financial collapse is historically low (a few percent). Absent evidence of a current severe structural crisis continuing through late 2025, the chance of another >5% drop closing-to-closing during the remainder of 2025 is estimated at around 12% to reflect both the baseline rarity of such drops and the higher volatility implied by the premise.
